_meta.source: heist-game-design.md "Scaling 2-7" + the-job-app-design.md §10.5; getawayBonus from heat-model-simulation.py player_bonus(n)

units.profiles.<n>.exhaustion: rotation strength class: full (5-7), light (4), tired (2-3 — soft class, no bench)
units.profiles.<n>.crewPerOption: [min,max] crew an obstacle option demands at this headcount
units.profiles.<n>.getawayBonus: odds bonus by headcount (sim player_bonus(n)); the getaway.headcountTerm in tuning.json multiplies it
units.exhaustionRest.<class>: rooms benched per exhaustion class after committing to an obstacle; tired=0 means no bench
units.minCommit.<gameId>: hard floor of committed crew before the dial is applied; the generator filters on this first
units.variant.<gameId>: id of a replacement game module used at certain commit sizes (silentSync collapses, no-trade, asymmetric-info)
units.dialCurve.<gameId>: how committed-crew lane ratings map to difficulty; tightenPerExtra eases the dial as headcount grows
units.dialCurve.perLanePoint: dial-level change per point of AVERAGE committed lane rating (wave 3: mean, not sum)
units.dialCurve.tightenPerExtraCrew: small residual easing per extra committed (player x lane) entry — more hands help a little
